title: Pasta failed error when running container with a privileged podman in container setup
body:
### Issue Description

KubeVirt uses a privileged podman in container setup to carry out a lot of CI testing. The containers.conf and other configuration files for this podman in container setup can be found here - https://github.com/kubevirt/project-infra/tree/main/images/bootstrap

This podman in container setup worked with podman v4 however it started to fail after upgrading to Fedora40 / podman v5. 

After upgrading to podman v5 the following error occurs when trying to run containers
```
podman run hello-world
Resolved "hello-world" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull quay.io/podman/hello:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 81df7ff16254 done   |
Copying config 5dd467fce5 done   |
Writing manifest to image destination
Error: setting up Pasta: pasta failed with exit code 1:
Don't run as root. Changing to nobody...
```



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. With rootful podman run the following - this image includes podman v5.0.3
    `podman run --privileged -e PODMAN_IN_CONTAINER_ENABLED=true quay.io/bcarey/bootstrap:v20240605-064d50c-amd64 podman run hello-world`





### Describe the results you received

The container fails to run.

### Describe the results you expected

The container runs successfully

For example with the same podman in container setup with podman v4.9.4:
```
podman run --privileged -e PODMAN_IN_CONTAINER_ENABLED=true quay.io/kubevirtci/bootstrap:v20240517-5a9a066 podman run hello-world
Enabling IPV6.
net.ipv6.conf.all.disable_ipv6 = 0
net.ipv6.conf.all.forwarding = 1
Podman in Container enabled, initializing in podman compatible mode...
Waiting for podman socket to be ready, sleeping for 1 seconds.
================================================================================
Done setting up podman in container.
fatal: not a git repository (or any of the parent directories): .git
+ podman run hello-world
Resolved "hello-world" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull quay.io/podman/hello:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:81df7ff16254ed9756e27c8de9ceb02a9568228fccadbf080f41cc5eb5118a44
Copying config sha256:5dd467fce50b56951185da365b5feee75409968cbab5767b9b59e325fb2ecbc0
Writing manifest to image destination
!... Hello Podman World ...!

         .--"--.           
       / -     - \         
      / (O)   (O) \        
   ~~~| -=(,Y,)=- |         
    .---. /`  \   |~~      
 ~/  o  o \~~~~.----. ~~   
  | =(X)= |~  / (O (O) \   
   ~~~~~~~  ~| =(Y_)=-  |   
  ~~~~    ~~~|   U      |~~ 

Project:   https://github.com/containers/podman
Website:   https://podman.io
Desktop:   https://podman-desktop.io
Documents: https://docs.podman.io
YouTube:   https://youtube.com/@Podman
X/Twitter: @Podman_io
Mastodon:  @Podman_io@fosstodon.org
+ EXIT_VALUE=0
+ set +o xtrace
Cleaning up after podman in container.
================================================================================
Cleaning up after podman
8e277cd053339cd5e287471aeeae1484253f15ecf081121b1631e66cf499e823
/usr/local/bin/runner.sh: line 50: wait: pid 8 is not a child of this shell
================================================================================
Done cleaning up after podman in container.
```

### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: Your outer and inner container is started as root (uid 0), right?

> Your outer and inner container is started as root (uid 0), right?

Yes that's correct. 

Outer container: 
```
[root@1358e56841ae workspace]# id 
uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root)
```

Please unset _CONTAINERS_USERNS_CONFIGURED from your image as workaround, see https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/22791

Or alternatively update your image to podman 5.1.1 it should contain the required bug fixes to make it work but honestly have no idea why _CONTAINERS_USERNS_CONFIGURED was ever added

> Please unset _CONTAINERS_USERNS_CONFIGURED from your image as workaround, see #22791
> 
> Or alternatively update your image to podman 5.1.1 it should contain the required bug fixes to make it work but honestly have no idea why _CONTAINERS_USERNS_CONFIGURED was ever added

That looks to work! Thanks @Luap99 !

We are just consuming the version shipped with Fedora for now so will try out this workaround for now. 

<details>

```
podman run --privileged -e PODMAN_IN_CONTAINER_ENABLED=true quay.io/bcarey/bootstrap:v20240605-064d50c-amd64 /bin/sh -c "unset _CONTAINERS_USERNS_CONFIGURED && podman run hello-world"
Enabling IPV6.
net.ipv6.conf.all.disable_ipv6 = 0
net.ipv6.conf.all.forwarding = 1
Podman in Container enabled, initializing in podman compatible mode...
Waiting for podman socket to be ready, sleeping for 1 seconds.
================================================================================
Done setting up podman in container.
fatal: not a git repository (or any of the parent directories): .git
+ /bin/sh -c 'unset _CONTAINERS_USERNS_CONFIGURED && podman run hello-world'
Resolved "hello-world" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull quay.io/podman/hello:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:81df7ff16254ed9756e27c8de9ceb02a9568228fccadbf080f41cc5eb5118a44
Copying config sha256:5dd467fce50b56951185da365b5feee75409968cbab5767b9b59e325fb2ecbc0
Writing manifest to image destination
!... Hello Podman World ...!

         .--"--.           
       / -     - \         
      / (O)   (O) \        
   ~~~| -=(,Y,)=- |         
    .---. /`  \   |~~      
 ~/  o  o \~~~~.----. ~~

Desktop:   https://podman-desktop.io
Documents: https://docs.podman.io
YouTube:   https://youtube.com/@Podman
X/Twitter: @Podman_io
Mastodon:  @Podman_io@fosstodon.org
```

</details>

You can drop the line here https://github.com/kubevirt/project-infra/blob/5fae7e4a6393bb9f06a33eae5ef32c3d8736213c/images/bootstrap/Dockerfile#L109

There is no harm in doing that and I really don't understand why this was ever added to our Containerfile in the first place.

But since the regression should be fixes in 5.1.1 I close this one.
